title: DrumBurp: Runtime language selector to Help menu
original_text: Let me check what's actually left to do: Phases 0–5 are all done. The only remaining automated work is Phase 6 — runtime language switching. The manual test (Phase 3) requires you to run the program. Let me implement Phase 6 now — a language selector in the Preferences area: Now I understand the full picture. The language selector needs to: 1. Be a menu action under &Help or &View — a submenu Language with one item per available .qm file 2. Save the chosen language to QSettings 3. Show a restart-required message (changing language mid-session is complex; saving + restart is the standard Qt approach) 4. Load the saved language at startup in DrumBurp.py before the UI is created. Let me implement this cleanly: Good. The language submenu goes under &View or &Help. I'll add it to &Help since it's a user preference. The implementation goes in DBMainwindow.py — build the submenu dynamically from available .qm files, save/load from QSettings: Now add the _buildLanguageMenu method and the on_actionSelectLanguage_triggered handler. Find a good place — after _finishLilyExport: Now add QActionGroup to the imports: Now update DrumBurp.py to read the saved language from QSettings before the UI is created:
expected_subject: feat(i18n): add runtime language selector to Help menu
expected_body_lines: - Implement dynamic submenu for available .qm translation files
- Persist user language preference via QSettings
- Load selected locale at startup before UI initialization
- Prompt user to restart application after language change
- Ensure seamless switching between English and Spanish
expected_command: git commit -m "feat(i18n): add runtime language selector to Help menu"
